questions.q: What was the primary profession of Mohammed Rafi in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi was one of the most celebrated playback singers in the history of Hindi cinema.

questions.q: In which year was the legendary playback singer Mohammed Rafi born?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi was born on 24 December 1924 in the village of Kotla Sultan Singh, in present-day Punjab.

questions.q: In which year did the playback singer Mohammed Rafi pass away?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi died on 31 July 1980 in Mumbai after a heart attack, at the age of 55.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi was primarily renowned as a playback voice in which language film industry?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi sang predominantly for Hindi films, though he also recorded songs in several other languages.

questions.q: With which veteran music composer did Mohammed Rafi share one of his most famous and enduring collaborations?
questions.explanation: Naushad was an early and frequent collaborator who helped establish Mohammed Rafi as a leading playback singer.

questions.q: For which broad quality was Mohammed Rafi most admired as a playback singer?
options: Singing only in a single fixed style
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi was celebrated for his versatility, singing romantic numbers, devotional songs, qawwalis and classical-based pieces with equal ease.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi was a male singer who provided the singing voice for which group of people on screen?
questions.explanation: As a playback singer, Mohammed Rafi lent his voice to numerous leading actors who lip-synced to his recordings on screen.

questions.q: Which type of devotional and spiritual songs is Mohammed Rafi especially remembered for rendering?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi was admired for his moving renditions of devotional songs, including Hindu bhajans and Islamic naats.

questions.q: In roughly which decade did Mohammed Rafi begin his playback singing career in Hindi films?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi's Hindi film playback career took off in the mid-1940s and continued until his death in 1980.

questions.q: Which national civilian honour was conferred upon Mohammed Rafi by the Government of India in 1967?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi was awarded the Padma Shri by the Government of India in 1967 in recognition of his contribution to music.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi frequently collaborated with which composer known for his work on films like 'Guide' and 'Pyaasa'?
questions.explanation: S. D. Burman was a major collaborator of Mohammed Rafi, with the singer voicing several hit songs for Dev Anand and others under his composition.

questions.q: Which famous composer duo, who scored many Shammi Kapoor films, worked extensively with Mohammed Rafi during the 1950s and 1960s?
questions.explanation: Shankar-Jaikishan were among Mohammed Rafi's most frequent collaborators, creating numerous hits for Shammi Kapoor and others during the 1950s and 1960s.

questions.q: For which Hindi actor did Mohammed Rafi famously provide the playback voice in many energetic 1960s hits like those in 'Junglee'?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi's voice became closely identified with Shammi Kapoor, including the famous 'Yahoo' song from Junglee.

questions.q: In which Indian state, in present-day Punjab, was Mohammed Rafi born?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi was born in the village of Kotla Sultan Singh in the Amritsar district of Punjab.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi recorded several memorable duets with which leading female playback singer of his era?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi and Lata Mangeshkar formed one of the most celebrated duet pairings in Hindi film music.

questions.q: The qawwali 'Parda Hai Parda', sung by Mohammed Rafi, appeared in which 1977 Amitabh Bachchan film?
questions.explanation: 'Parda Hai Parda' from Amar Akbar Anthony (1977), composed by Laxmikant-Pyarelal, is one of Mohammed Rafi's best-known qawwalis.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi sang the patriotic song 'Kar Chale Hum Fida' featured in which war-themed film?
questions.explanation: 'Kar Chale Hum Fida' from the film Haqeeqat (1964) is a celebrated patriotic song rendered by Mohammed Rafi.

questions.q: In which competitive category did Mohammed Rafi win the Filmfare Award six times over his career?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi won the Filmfare Award for Best Male Playback Singer six times, including for songs such as 'Chaudhvin Ka Chand Ho' and 'Kya Hua Tera Wada'.

questions.q: Apart from Hindi, Mohammed Rafi also recorded songs in which of the following languages?
options: Only Hindi and no other language
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi sang in many languages including Punjabi, Bengali, Marathi and others, in addition to Hindi and Urdu.

questions.q: Which iconic song from the film 'Pyaasa' (1957) features the voice of Mohammed Rafi?
options: Yeh Duniya Agar Mil Bhi Jaye
questions.explanation: 'Yeh Duniya Agar Mil Bhi Jaye' from Pyaasa, composed by S. D. Burman, is a landmark song sung by Mohammed Rafi.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi sang the song 'Chaudhvin Ka Chand Ho' picturised on Guru Dutt in a film of the same name from which decade?
questions.explanation: 'Chaudhvin Ka Chand Ho' is from the 1960 film Chaudhvin Ka Chand, with music by Ravi.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi was a contemporary and friendly rival of which other dominant male playback singer of Hindi cinema?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi and Kishore Kumar were the two dominant male playback voices, with Kishore Kumar rising to prominence in the 1970s.

questions.q: Which song earned Mohammed Rafi his first Filmfare Award for Best Male Playback Singer in 1961?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi won his first Filmfare Best Male Playback Singer award for the title song 'Chaudhvin Ka Chand Ho'.

questions.q: For which 1977 film's song did Mohammed Rafi receive the National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi won the National Film Award for the song 'Kya Hua Tera Wada' from Hum Kisise Kum Naheen (1977).

questions.q: In which Naushad-composed film of the 1950s did Mohammed Rafi sing several classical-based songs for Dilip Kumar, including 'Madhuban Mein Radhika'?
questions.explanation: 'Madhuban Mein Radhika Nache Re' from Kohinoor (1960), composed by Naushad, is a classical-based song picturised on Dilip Kumar.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi sang the song 'O Duniya Ke Rakhwale' in which 1952 Naushad-scored film built around a singer's life?
questions.explanation: 'O Duniya Ke Rakhwale' from Baiju Bawra (1952) is among Mohammed Rafi's most demanding classical-based renditions for Naushad.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi's song 'Chahunga Main Tujhe Saanjh Savere' won a Filmfare award and appeared in which 1964 film?
questions.explanation: 'Chahunga Main Tujhe' is from Dosti (1964), with music by Laxmikant-Pyarelal, and won Mohammed Rafi a Filmfare Award.

questions.q: Which composer, known for ghazal-style melodies, worked with Mohammed Rafi on songs such as those in 'Heer Raanjha'?
questions.explanation: Madan Mohan collaborated with Mohammed Rafi on several songs, including the music of Heer Raanjha (1970).

questions.q: The hit song 'Chaudhvin Ka Chand Ho' and 'Yeh Duniya Agar Mil Bhi Jaye' were both picturised on which actor-director that Mohammed Rafi frequently voiced?
questions.explanation: Both songs were picturised on Guru Dutt, for whom Mohammed Rafi provided several memorable playback renditions.

questions.q: Mohammed Rafi sang the popular romantic song 'Baharon Phool Barsao' for Shammi Kapoor in which 1966 film?
questions.explanation: 'Baharon Phool Barsao' is from the 1966 film Suraj, composed by Shankar-Jaikishan, and won Mohammed Rafi a Filmfare Award.
